truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 identified 7 of the 20 true vulnerabilities; all were correctly supported by exact source lines. 13 additional vulnerabilities were missed and not mentioned.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Runtime Logging
Items.Notes: The format_string function uses snprintf with a user‑supplied fmt and the runtime log format is set via set_runtime_log_format, matching the vulnerability.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The factorial implementation in FunctionRegistry multiplies a long long result by i without overflow checks; this integer overflow was not mentioned in the audit.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The cleanup method iterates over blocks and frees them, yet a block could be freed while still in use; the audit did not flag this use‑after‑free.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: OS Command Injection in Config Validation
Items.Notes: The validate_config method builds a command string from user‑controlled inputs and executes it via system(), matching the true vulnerability.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Off-by-One Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression
Items.Notes: The evaluate_expression function copies a 1024‑byte expression into a fixed buffer without checking for length > 1023, causing a stack overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The increment operation reads old_value from value_ and then updates it under a lock, but the read itself is not atomic, creating a data race not mentioned.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Authentication Bypass via Emergency Override
Items.Notes: The authenticate method checks for EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE after the login attempt counter exceeds 5, granting access without a password – exactly SC‑V3‑007.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The ConfigLoader::load_config method builds full_path = config_dir_ + filename and opens it without sanitizing the filename, a classic path traversal issue.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser
Items.Notes: ExpressionParser::parse concatenates all token text into expr_str and copies it into a fixed 256‑byte buffer with strcpy, causing a heap overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hard-Coded Administrative Secret
Items.Notes: A constexpr ADMIN_SECRET array containing the literal password is present, matching the true vulnerability.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The session token generator uses a 4‑digit range (1000‑9999) seeded by a random device, which is insufficient for strong randomness.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The static cached_time in log_debug_message is written without any lock, leading to a data race.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The emergency audit log is written using a shell command that appends to a temporary file, an insecure practice not flagged.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The admin console keeps login_attempts_ and authenticated_ as static globals, so the authentication state survives process restarts.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The variable parsing code calls std::stod without exception handling, leaving a potential unhandled exception.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The memory pool split calculation does not guard against integer underflow when previous_size is close to the requested size plus metadata.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: ExpressionParser's recursive parsing has no maximum depth check, despite the presence of MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH in config.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The result_cache_ unordered_map is read and written concurrently without any mutex, causing a data race.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The config loader checks std::filesystem::exists, then sleeps for 25 ms before opening, creating a TOCTOU vulnerability.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: All findings are supported by exact source lines.
Items.Notes: The route scoring function performs integer division by token_length without checking for zero, leading to undefined behavior.

Items.Key: arbitrary command execution via admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated admin users can run any shell command, giving full host control.|admin console receives an exec <command> instruction after successful authentication.|replace system() with a sandboxed execution mechanism (e.g., execve with restricted environment) and validate the command.->arbitrary command execution via admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated admin users can run any shell command, giving full host control.|admin console receives an exec <command> instruction after successful authentication.|replace system() with a sandboxed execution mechanism (e.g., execve with restricted environment) and validate the command.

os command injection in emergency audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|| void append_override_audit_event() { #ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str()); }|after bypassing authentication, the emergency audit event writes to a log file using a shell command where the path is derived from environment variables, allowing arbitrary file writes and command execution.|an authenticated session (via emergency_override bypass) calls append_override_audit_event.|use a dedicated file i/o api (e.g., std::ofstream) instead of system() to write the audit log, and never invoke a shell with user-controlled paths.
